steps.text: Set up: Use a large outdoor boil pot with basket, burner, and propane. Fill pot about 2/3 with water (leave room for displacement). Bring to a rolling boil.
Season the boil: Add Cajun boil seasoning, optional liquid concentrate, bay leaves, lemons, garlic, and onions. Boil 10–15 minutes to build a flavorful boil.
Cook potatoes first: Add potatoes (and optional artichokes if using). Boil until potatoes are nearly tender.
Add sausage and corn: Add sausage, corn, and optional mushrooms. Return to a boil and cook until corn is tender and sausage is hot.

Rinse crawfish: While vegetables cook, rinse live crawfish in clean water until rinse water runs clearer. Purging with salt is optional and controversial; if you choose to purge, do it briefly and rinse thoroughly.
Add crawfish: Turn heat up to maintain a strong boil. Add crawfish to the basket and lower into the pot. Return to a boil and cook briefly.

Kill the boil, then soak: After the crawfish come back to a boil, cook a few minutes, then turn off the heat. Let everything soak in the seasoned water so crawfish take on flavor. Taste a crawfish during the soak and adjust soak time to preference.
Optional spice adjustment: If you want more heat/flavor, add more boil seasoning or concentrate during the soak (small amounts at a time). Stir carefully and re-taste.
Drain and dump: Lift the basket and let it drain well. Dump crawfish, corn, potatoes, sausage, and add-ins onto a covered table (or large trays).
Serve: Serve immediately with optional melted butter and hot sauce. Provide paper towels, gloves, and plenty of cold drinks.
